subject: RE: Conversation
body:
Thank you for your synopsis of Mr. Molyneux's concerns about my
information request.    I am rather saddened with the responses I have
received from you over the past two weeks regarding information I would
like to provide to Mr. Epstein.  I have never before been told from a
vendor that I cannot have details for something we have purchased or
made deposits on.  Then when photographs and financial data are
requested I am told that they will take two weeks to obtain.  

 

Doug,  earlier today I asked you  for backup of the all expenses on the
Fancelli summary and you stated that you cannot provide this information
as it will consume countless hours of your time.  I then mentioned that
Fancelli would be providing this information and you told me that no one
has ever asked for this and everything you have provided to date is more
than any other client has ever received.  What concerns me about your
inability to present this backup is the true accuracy of what you have
already provided to me. It would seem logical that all that backup of
these expenses were required in order to prepare the summary I received.
Unfortunately it is this type of communication that has led to the trust
issue here.

 

I strongly believe that Mr. Epstein is entitled to a detailed analysis
of work performed to date for the library and pool woodworking deposits
he made in December 2006.   In addition to detailed invoices I would
like to see a copy of your wire transfer documents evidencing the two
wires to Fancelli in the amounts of 1,050,000 and 379,500 Euros.

 

Please feel free to contact me with any questions.

 

Thank you

 

 

Rich Kahn

New York Strategy Group LLC

457 Madison Avenue, 4th Floor

New York, NY 10021

tel 212-891-6430

fax 212-750-2408

cell 917-414-7584



 

From: Doug Stine [mailto:doug@molyneuxstudio.com] 
Sent: Friday, February 15, 2008 12:33 PM
To: Rich Kahn
Cc: jpm6929@aol.com; Pilar Molyneux
Subject: Conversation

 

Dear Rich

 

Mr. Molyneux is currently in Paris going into the Elysee Palace but he
wanted me to reiterate our concerns about your request

1.	This is something that we do not do at Molyneux Studio (supply
backup documents for vendors budgets).  The list that was given to you
is even more than what we have done in the past for other clients.
2.	If the project is not being cancelled why is this documentation
necessary since the whole amount will be used at one point or another?
3.	There seems to be a trust issue.

 

Best

Doug Stine

 

JP Molyneux Studio, LTD.

29 East 69th Street

New York, NY 10021

 

Phone: 212.628.0097

Fax: 212.737.6126

 

doug@molyneuxstudio.com <mailto:ferrari@molyneuxstudio.com>
body_html:
Thank you for your synopsis of Mr. Molyneux’s concerns about my information request.    I am rather saddened with the responses I have received from you over the past two weeks regarding information I would like to provide to Mr. Epstein.  I have never before been told from a vendor that I cannot have details for something we have purchased or made deposits on.  Then when photographs and financial data are requested I am told that they will take two weeks to obtain. 

 

Doug,  earlier today I asked you  for backup of the all expenses on the Fancelli summary and you stated that you cannot provide this information as it will consume countless hours of your time.  I then mentioned that Fancelli would be providing this information and you told me that no one has ever asked for this and everything you have provided to date is more than any other client has ever received.  What concerns me about your inability to present this backup is the true accuracy of what you have already provided to me. It would seem logical that all that backup of these expenses were required in order to prepare the summary I received.  Unfortunately it is this type of communication that has led to the trust issue here.

 

I strongly believe that Mr. Epstein is entitled to a detailed analysis of work performed to date for the library and pool woodworking deposits he made in December 2006.   In addition to detailed invoices I would like to see a copy of your wire transfer documents evidencing the two wires to Fancelli in the amounts of 1,050,000 and 379,500 Euros.

 

Please feel free to contact me with any questions.

 

Thank you

 

 

Rich Kahn

New York Strategy Group LLC

New York, NY 10021

fax 212-750-2408

From: Doug Stine [mailto:doug@molyneuxstudio.com]
Sent: Friday, February 15, 2008 12:33 PM
To: Rich Kahn
Cc: jpm6929@aol.com; Pilar Molyneux
Subject: Conversation

 

Dear Rich

 

Mr. Molyneux is currently in Paris going into the Elysee Palace but he wanted me to reiterate our concerns about your request

  1. This is something that we do not do at Molyneux Studio (supply backup documents for vendors budgets).  The list that was given to you is even more than what we have done in the past for other clients.
  2. If the project is not being cancelled why is this documentation necessary since the whole amount will be used at one point or another?
  3. There seems to be a trust issue.

 

Best

Doug Stine
